defects.symptom: Committed/shipped dummyindex files fail standard pre-commit hygiene hooks (end-of-file-fixer, trailing-whitespace) in consumer repos: .context/features/symbol-graph.json lacks a final newline, the installed .claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version lacks a final newline, and the vendored .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/10-structural-review.md has trailing whitespace (evidence item 84; reproduced at HEAD via fresh `python3 -m dummyindex ingest` into a mktemp repo: features/symbol-graph.json is the only committable artifact missing EOF newline).
defects.root_cause:
export/graph.py:38 writes symbol-graph.json with `json.dump(data, f, indent=2)` — the ONLY JSON writer in the package that does not append "
" (every other writer uses `json.dumps(...) + "
"`, e.g. features/helpers.py:131-134, build/maps.py:288, source_docs/writers.py:21). installer/install.py:143 writes `.dummyindex_version` as `PACKAGE_VERSION` with no trailing newline (no code reads its content — only test_install.py:114 which already .strip()s, so adding 
 is safe). skills/council/10-structural-review.md carries 6 empty-blockquote lines written as '> ' (blockquote marker + trailing space); the installer copies council/*.md verbatim into consumer repos (install.py:91-92) where they get committed and trip the trailing-whitespace hook.
defects.proposed_fix:
(1) export/graph.py: after `json.dump(data, f, indent=2)` add `f.write("
")` (or refactor to the codebase-standard `path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "
")` atomic-tmp pattern). (2) installer/install.py:143: write `PACKAGE_VERSION + "
"`. (3) Strip the trailing space from the six '> ' lines in skills/council/10-structural-review.md ('> ' -> '>'; pure cosmetic, blockquote rendering unchanged). (4) Add a repo-level packaging-hygiene test that walks dummyindex/skills/** (every file the installer ships verbatim) asserting no line has trailing whitespace and every non-empty file ends with a newline — this locks the whole shipped tree against regressions, not just the one file.
defects.tests_to_add:
tests/context/build/test_graph.py: assert the written symbol-graph.json bytes end with b'
' (idiom already used at tests/context/output/test_docs.py:145 `assert text.endswith("
")`). tests/test_install.py: extend test_install_project_scope_writes_version_stamp to assert version_file.read_text().endswith("
"). New tests/test_skills_hygiene.py: parametrized walk of dummyindex/skills/** asserting no trailing whitespace on any line and EOF newline on every file.

defects.symptom: detect-secrets 'Hex High Entropy String' fails consumer CI with ~140 hits on committed dummyindex artifacts: per-file sha256 hashes in .context/map/files.json, sha256 in .context/source-docs/INDEX.json, and agent-written 64-hex features/<id>/.hash files. Consumer sessions resolve it by blanket-excluding ^.context/ from ALL pre-commit hooks, losing every check on the tree (evidence items 83, 85; reproduced at HEAD: fresh ingest emits 40+-char hex strings in map/files.json and source-docs/INDEX.json).
defects.root_cause: dummyindex embeds full sha256 hex content-hashes in committed JSON (maps.py:268 emits one per indexed source file — hence ~140 hits on a ~1000-file repo; source_docs/models.py:35 one per doc) and the council resume protocol (19-resume.md:51-54) has agents write bare 64-hex .hash files that are committed (confirmed tracked in BOS-Mono backend). Nothing in the package mentions pre-commit or detect-secrets (grep: zero hits outside equip's formatter-detection list), so every consumer repo with secret scanning hits a red CI wall and invents a blanket exclude ad hoc. Note: the focus's 'shorter hashes' idea does NOT work — detect-secrets' HexHighEntropyString plugin uses a Shannon-entropy threshold of 3.0, and even a 16-char random hex string has entropy ~3.5; also the hashes are compared for equality in incremental.py:112-129 and manifest.py:186 so they must stay collision-resistant. Exclusion guidance is the correct fix, not a layout change.
defects.proposed_fix: Ship scanner-coexistence guidance instead of changing the hash layout: (1) instructions.py (HOW_TO_USE.md template): add a short 'CI / lint / secret-scanning' section documenting that map/files.json, source-docs/INDEX.json, features/symbol-graph.json, features/graph.json and features/<id>/.hash are machine-generated and regenerated wholesale, with a recommended NARROW pre-commit `exclude:` regex scoped to those paths (e.g. ^\.context/(map/|source-docs/INDEX\.json|features/(symbol-graph|graph)\.(json|html)|features/[^/]+/\.hash)) — explicitly steering users away from blanket-excluding all of .context/ (curated feature docs and conventions SHOULD stay linted). (2) CLI-boundary nicety: in `context init` / install auto-init (installer/install.py _auto_init_project or cli/init.py), after a successful build, detect `.pre-commit-config.yaml` or `.secrets.baseline` at the repo root (read-only check) and print a one-time stdout notice pointing at the HOW_TO_USE section with the snippet — print-only, never edit the consumer's config (design decision confirmed: auto-editing a consumer's pre-commit config is too invasive; stdout + docs is the seam). (3) council/19-resume.md: change the .hash write instruction to include a trailing newline (fixes its end-of-file-fixer hit too).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/output/test_instructions.py: assert the rendered HOW_TO_USE contains the CI/secret-scanning section and the exclude regex mentions map/ and symbol-graph. tests for the init-time notice: build into tmp repo containing a .pre-commit-config.yaml, capture stdout, assert the notice appears; assert absent when no pre-commit config exists.

defects.symptom: Repos upgraded from older dummyindex never receive the expanded managed .context/.gitignore: scratch artifacts (_reality-check.md, _council-log.json, legacy root-level _enrich_plan.json, _structural-plan.json) stay unignored, get committed, and trip CI hooks; the legacy 392KB .context/_enrich_plan.json survives upgrades with no cleanup. Confirmed live: BOS-Mono/backend/.context is meta-stamped dummyindex_version 0.25.0 (refreshed 2026-06-12) yet its .gitignore still has only the pre-0.15.3 two patterns (cache/, _doc_backups/) — and its committed features/*/_reality-check.md files were exactly what failed the CI run in evidence item 84.
locations: dummyindex/context/build/incremental.py:137-155 (curated repos always take the refresh branch)
defects.root_cause: _ensure_context_gitignore (runner.py:324) — which both creates the managed .gitignore AND appends newly-added managed patterns on upgrade — is called only from _write_all (runner.py:362), which runs only on a full destructive build_all. Curated/enriched repos are deliberately routed away from build_all by the non-destructive guard (incremental.py:137) into refresh_deterministic_artifacts (enriched_refresh.py), which never touches the gitignore. So the exact repos that upgrade in place (every curated index) can never receive the patterns added in 0.15.3+ (_enrich_plan.json, _structural-plan.json, _council-log.json, _reality-check.{json,md}). The runner.py:301-303 comment ('belt-and-suspenders: repos indexed by 0.15.0 may still have a transitional copy at the .context/ root') is wishful — the belt never reaches those repos. Additionally nothing ever deletes a legacy root-level .context/_enrich_plan.json left by pre-999dc08 versions.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Move _MANAGED_GITIGNORE_PATTERNS + _CONTEXT_GITIGNORE_HEADER + _ensure_context_gitignore into a small shared module (e.g. dummyindex/context/build/gitignore.py — repo convention: many small files; keep re-exports in runner.py so tests/context/build/test_no_cache_leak.py imports keep working, or update its imports). (2) Call _ensure_context_gitignore(context_dir) at the top of refresh_deterministic_artifacts (enriched_refresh.py:~90, right after context_dir is derived) — it is deterministic, append-only-missing, preserves user lines (runner.py:338-344), so it fits the module's never-touch-enrichment contract; no import cycle (runner does not import enriched_refresh). (3) Migration cleanup in the same refresh path: if `.context/_enrich_plan.json` exists at the root (legacy location), unlink it — it is a regenerable scratch work-list by definition (new runs write under cache/, cli/enrich.py:33). Design note for the implementer: only delete this one known-legacy filename, never glob; and document in the docstring that already-TRACKED scratch files in consumer repos need a one-time `git rm --cached` which dummyindex must not do itself (mention it in the HOW_TO_USE CI section from the detect-secrets defect).
defects.tests_to_add: Extend tests/context/build/test_no_cache_leak.py: (a) seed a tmp .context with the legacy two-pattern .gitignore and a curated features/INDEX.json, run refresh_deterministic_artifacts, assert all _MANAGED_GITIGNORE_PATTERNS present and user lines preserved; (b) seed a root-level .context/_enrich_plan.json, run refresh, assert it is gone while cache/_enrich_plan.json (if present) is untouched; (c) assert idempotency of the refresh-path gitignore upgrade.

defects.symptom: `dummyindex install --scope project` ships 9 inert *.md.tmpl render templates into the consumer repo at .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/, which the equip renderer never reads; they get committed (confirmed: 9 tracked .tmpl files in BOS-Mono backend, NOT covered by the session's ^.claude/skills/dummyindex/ pre-commit exclude), pollute reconcile's unassigned-files list, mislead agents about blast radius, and silently reappear after deletion on the next install (evidence items 10, 61).
defects.root_cause: install.py:132-140 copies a uniform companion tuple `("templates", "agents")` for every sibling skill. But equip's templates/ are render-time inputs the CLI always resolves from the installed package — render.py:38 hardcodes `_TEMPLATES_DIR = Path(_skills_pkg.__file__).resolve().parent / "equip" / "templates"` — and equip's SKILL.md never references a templates/ path, so the repo copy is dead weight. By contrast audit's agents/ companion is genuinely read at runtime by the conductor (audit SKILL.md:49 instructs reading agents/<persona_id>.md next to the SKILL.md), so the companion mechanism itself must stay. Currently only two companion dirs exist in the package (equip/templates, audit/agents); plan/build/update have none.
defects.proposed_fix: Replace the uniform companion tuple with a per-skill companion map in the sibling-skill loop, e.g. extend the existing `for sub_name, skill_label in (...)` tuples to `(sub_name, skill_label, companions)` with `("equip", "dummyindex-equip", ())` and `("audit", "dummyindex-audit", ("agents",))`. Add an upgrade purge mirroring the v0.14 stale-markdown wipe precedent (install.py:89-90): when installing the equip sibling skill, if `<dst>/templates/` exists from a prior version, remove its *.tmpl files (and the dir if then empty) so upgrades clean up the dead weight instead of re-shipping it. Design decision for the implementer: deleting the stale templates dir on upgrade is safe because the renderer provably never reads it and the files are byte-identical package copies — but keep the purge scoped to *.tmpl inside templates/ only, never user files. Also update the install.py:127-131 comment that claims companions 'mirror the pyproject package-data globs' — package-data must still ship templates (the renderer needs them in site-packages); only the repo copy goes away.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_install.py: (a) test_install_does_not_ship_equip_templates — install(scope="project"), assert no *.tmpl exists under <tmp>/.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/; (b) test_install_still_ships_audit_agents — assert .claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/agents/*.md present; (c) test_install_upgrade_purges_stale_equip_templates — pre-seed <dst>/templates/x.md.tmpl, reinstall, assert gone (mirrors existing test_install_upgrade_purges_stale_companion_markdowns at tests/test_install.py:166).

defects.symptom: Giant deterministic artifacts are committed by default and rewritten wholesale on every refresh, producing 113k-line diffs and churn: features/symbol-graph.json is 4.9MB in BOS-Mono backend (tracked), features/graph.json 756K (tracked), map/symbols.json 432K (tracked) — and the reconcile doc explicitly instructs `git add .context` wholesale, so every reconcile commit drags megabytes of regenerated JSON with the curated docs (evidence focus item 3; symbol-graph churn).
locations: dummyindex/context/build/enriched_refresh.py:108-145 (rewrites map/*, source-docs INDEX, symbol-graph on every refresh)
dummyindex/export/graph.py:38 (indent=2 pretty-printing multiplies symbol-graph size ~node-link verbosity)
dummyindex/context/build/runner.py:297-309 (managed gitignore has no entry for any of these)
defects.root_cause: There is no commit-policy distinction between the curated taxonomy (features/<id>/*.md, feature.json, conventions/, PROJECT.md, HOW_TO_USE.md — small, hand-enriched, must be committed) and the regenerable machine layer (features/symbol-graph.json, features/graph.json, features/graph.html, map/*.json — fully deterministic outputs of `rebuild --changed`). The managed .gitignore ignores only scratch files; the docs (65-reconcile.md:158) say `git add .context` with no carve-out; and symbol-graph.json is additionally pretty-printed (indent=2) which inflates a full node-link dump of every symbol+edge. Consequence confirmed in the consumer repo: all four artifact classes are git-tracked.
defects.proposed_fix: This is a design decision the implementer must surface to the owner; recommended two-step: (STEP 1, safe now) keep the artifacts committed but emit a managed `.context/.gitattributes` next to the managed .gitignore (same _ensure_* upgrade-merge pattern, ideally in the new gitignore.py module) marking the machine layer `linguist-generated=true -diff merge=ours` (lines for features/symbol-graph.json, features/graph.json, features/graph.html, map/*.json, source-docs/INDEX.json) so GitHub collapses the diffs and reviews stop drowning; ALSO compact-print symbol-graph.json (indent=None, separators=(",", ":")) — it is machine-read only (reality_check.py:336-342, feature scaffolder), cutting size several-fold, while still ending with the newline from the EOF defect fix. Update 65-reconcile.md step 5 to mention the generated layer is diff-suppressed. (STEP 2, opt-in later) a `.context/config.json` flag (e.g. "commit_machine_artifacts": false) that adds the machine layer to the managed gitignore for teams that prefer regeneration-on-clone — NOT the default, because fresh clones would lose query/reality-check inputs until a rebuild runs. Do not silently change the default commit behavior in a patch release.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_no_cache_leak.py (or new test_gitattributes.py): build into tmp dir, assert .context/.gitattributes exists, contains linguist-generated lines for symbol-graph/graph/map, and that the upgrade-merge preserves user lines. tests/context/build/test_graph.py: assert symbol-graph.json parses and contains no two-space indentation (compact form) and ends with newline.

defects.symptom: Council-generated feature spec.md used a realistic GitHub-PAT-shaped placeholder (`"api_key": "ghp_..."`), which detect-secrets flagged as a Secret Keyword in consumer CI (raw-findings: .context/features/integrations-oauth/spec.md:153). Nothing in the council templates tells the drafting agent to use obviously-fake placeholders.
locations: dummyindex/skills/council/20-specify.md (spec-drafting stage; no placeholder rule — grep for placeholder/fake/secret across dummyindex/skills/council/*.md and skills/skill.md returns zero hits)
dummyindex/skills/agents/dev.md (the persona whose body drafts spec.md)
defects.root_cause: The spec-drafting instructions (council/20-specify.md + agents/dev.md persona body) contain no output-hygiene rule about example credentials, so devs naturally write provider-realistic placeholders (ghp_, sk-, AKIA…) in example request bodies — exactly the prefixes secret scanners pattern-match. Confirmed absent at HEAD: no placeholder/secret guidance anywhere in the shipped council/agent markdown.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a short, verbatim-includable directive to council/20-specify.md (alongside the existing 'Doc-evidence directive') and to the dev persona body in skills/agents/dev.md: 'Example values in specs must be obviously fake and scanner-safe: use angle-bracket placeholders (<YOUR_API_KEY>) or the literal string "example-not-a-real-key"; never use provider-shaped prefixes (ghp_, gho_, sk-, AKIA, xoxb-, -----BEGIN) even truncated — secret scanners pattern-match the prefix, not the validity.' Keep it to ~3 lines so the prompt-size cost is negligible.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_skills_hygiene.py (from the whitespace defect): add an assertion that no file under dummyindex/skills/ contains the literal scanner-bait prefixes (ghp_, sk-live, AKIA, xoxb-) outside the directive line itself — guards the templates from regressing with realistic examples.

quick_wins: dummyindex/export/graph.py:38 — add `f.write("\n")` after json.dump (1 line; fixes the only EOF-newline violation a fresh HEAD ingest still produces).
dummyindex/installer/install.py:143 — `write_text(PACKAGE_VERSION + "\n")` (1 line; no code reads the stamp's content, and tests/test_install.py:114 already strips).
dummyindex/skills/council/10-structural-review.md — strip trailing space from the six '> ' empty-blockquote lines (42, 44, 46, 48, 77, 79).
dummyindex/skills/council/19-resume.md:51-54 — amend the .hash write instruction to end the file with a newline (e.g. 'write the 64-hex digest followed by a newline').

Local dev-repo cleanup (untracked, gitignored): `rm -rf /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/build /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex.egg-info` — the egg-info advertises 0.21.0 vs pyproject 0.25.0 and build/lib is the classic stale-wheel trap; optionally add a `rm -rf build *.egg-info` step to scripts/release.py or CONTRIBUTING.md.
dummyindex/pipeline/io/cache.py:97 — `json.dumps(result) + "\n"` for writer consistency (cache/ is gitignored, cosmetic only).
not_confirmed: Evidence item 18 (ephemeral _enrich_plan.json left at .context/ root and not gitignored): ALREADY FIXED at HEAD by commit 999dc08 'fix(context): move enrich-plan work-list to cache/ and gitignore internal scratch' — cli/enrich.py:33 now writes .context/cache/_enrich_plan.json and runner.py:304 carries a belt-and-suspenders ignore pattern. The residual gap (the upgraded gitignore never reaching curated repos, and no cleanup of the legacy 392KB root copy) is covered by the gitignore-upgrade defect above.

'.pending-enrichment markers persist after enrichment' (focus item 2b): no code defect confirmed. The marker lifecycle is by design — tracked on purpose (features/constants.py:31 'restart-safe'), written by reconcile placements (placement.py:381-389), cleared idempotently by `dummyindex context mark-enriched` (cli/features.py:444-485 → placement.py:355-378), and reconcile-stamp refuses while any remain (council/65-reconcile.md:43). The live BOS-Mono backend tree (0.25.0) contains ZERO stale markers. Persistence in evidence sessions was workflow non-adherence (agents skipping mark-enriched), which belongs to the reconcile-workflow cluster.

'committed dummyindex.egg-info pinned at 0.21.0 + stale build/lib tree' (focus item 4a, evidence item 100): NOT committed — `git ls-files` matches nothing; both are gitignored (.gitignore `*.egg-info/`, `/build/`). They are stale on the local filesystem only (egg-info PKG-INFO says 0.21.0). Demoted to a quick-win local cleanup.

'~40 generated .context files fail trailing-whitespace and EOF hooks' (evidence item 84) — only PARTIALLY reproducible at HEAD: every feature/INDEX/flow/reality-check/source-docs JSON+MD writer now appends '\n' (features/helpers.py:131-134, reality_check.py:451/485/497, source_docs/writers.py:21, build/{maps,tree,meta,manifest,conventions}.py), and a fresh HEAD ingest produces zero trailing-whitespace files. The BOS-Mono offenders feature.json/INDEX.json missing newlines were written by older dummyindex versions or agent hand-edits (evidence item 44 documents hand-built feature.json). What remains at HEAD is exactly the three locations in the whitespace/EOF defect: symbol-graph.json, .dummyindex_version, council/10-structural-review.md (plus agent-written .hash files via the 19-resume.md instruction).
